MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-05-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, LAI NG MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied. "The papers are to be construed strictly against the moving party and liberally in favor of the opposing party; any doubts regarding the propriety of summary judgment are to be resolved in favor of the opposing party." (Kulesa v. Castleberry (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 103, 112.) In this case, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff was within the course and scope of her employment when she boarded Mr. Ng's light rail vehicle and injured herself.

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Vedica Puri, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
